channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump officials list 5 weekly accomplishments ahead of Musk midnight deadline
item.description: Some Trump officials are listing their own accomplishments for last week ahead of a deadline on Monday evening for federal employees to report what they achieved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem ends Biden-era use of controversial app to allow migrants to board flights, except to self-deport
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem is ending the use of the CBP One app to allow migrants to board flights, the latest turn from the Biden-era use of the app.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel is sworn in as acting ATF chief, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New FBI Director Kash Patel was sworn in Monday as acting chief of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, taking the helm of two separate and sprawling Justice Department agencies, according to a person familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rescinds Biden order protecting gender clinics from investigation, signals new whistleblower protections
item.description: The Trump administration rescinded Biden-era guidance protecting hospitals and clinics from complying with investigations related to transgender medical treatments, particularly to minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal workers return to offices amid threat from Elon Musk
item.description: Federal employees across the country, many of whom have worked from home since the COVID-19 pandemic, were back at agency offices Monday under President Donald Trump’s return-to-office mandate.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Cabinet members have already backtracked on some promises made before being confirmed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As they mustered support for their confirmations by the U.S. Senate, some of President Donald Trump's appointees made statements from which they've already distanced themselves upon taking office.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secret Service agent who attempted to shield JFK from assassination dead at 93
item.description: Clint Hill, the Secret Service agent who jumped into action to attempt to help President John F. Kennedy during his assassination, has died at the age of 93.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flashback: Musk used his 'what did you get done this week' directive before gutting Twitter
item.description: Tech billionaire and DOGE Chair Elon Musk previously used his "what did you get done this week" question with the former CEO of Twitter before Musk purchased and gutted the social media giant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Legal expert: Federal employee safeguards moving too slowly to protect workers
item.description: Federal employees filed a lawsuit over an email telling them to list their accomplishments from the last week and a warning from Elon Musk that if they do not respond, they could lose their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump immigration policy allowing arrests in churches for some religious groups
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday blocked immigration agents from conducting enforcement operations in houses of worship for Quakers and a handful of other religious groups. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Feds target “criminals and illegal immigrants” in Houston-area development, Abbott says
item.description: The Colony Ridge development has long been under scrutiny from conservative lawmakers, think tanks and influencers who cast it as a hotbed for undocumented immigrants and cartel crime.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandro Serrano, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: US pushes UN resolution on Russia-Ukraine war that omits mention of Moscow’s aggression
item.description: In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Republicans’ love/hate relationship with the Education Department
item.description: President Trump has made clear he wants to close the U.S. Department of Education, but Republicans seem torn on just how far to go.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Greenpeace faces a $300 million lawsuit after Dakota Access Pipeline protests
item.description: The company behind the controversial Dakota Access Pipeline is suing Greenpeace for at least $300 million for damages the oil pipeline company says it suffered from protests in 2016 and 2017.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: Millionaire Vivek Ramaswamy set to join the Ohio governor's race
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vivek Ramaswamy, the Cincinnati-born biotech entrepreneur who departed the Department of Government Efficiency initiative on President Donald Trump's first day, was expected to launch his bid for Ohio governor Monday.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2,000 USAID staff to be cut, others put on administrative leave
item.description: “There is no one left to do any work,” the USAID official said, requesting anonymity to protect against retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump fires top US general in unprecedented Pentagon shakeup
item.description: A U.S. official said Brown was relieved with immediate effect, even before the Senate confirms his successor.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: PIX Panel discusses Hochul’s safeguard plan for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday announced proposed safeguards to ensure that New York City is operating in the best interest of its residents.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump’s second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
